content.text: Test coverage is good, continue maintaining 80%+ coverage
Avoid editing runtime/ directly - edit .aether/ instead
avoid direct runtime edits
Learning captured: Use explicit jq if/elif chains instead of the // operator when checking fields that can legitimately be false
Learning captured: Use explicit jq if/elif chains instead of the // operator when checking fields that can legitimately be false
Focus on error handling in auth module

Phase 1 (Security Foundation) completed. Key patterns: multi-word regex for injection detection, 8000-char token budget, decayed effective_strength for eternal promotion, SHA-256 dedup with reinforce-on-collision
Learning captured: Prompt injection regex needs multi-word support with optional groups
Learning captured: Colony-prime token budget 8000 chars matches measured output of 2000-5000 chars
Phase 2 (Queen Memory) completed. Key patterns: dual-function lockstep updates for _extract_wisdom, template placeholder detection needed, section header guards prevent content leakage
Learning captured: Two wisdom extraction functions must be updated in lockstep
Phase 3 (Hive Reads) completed. Key patterns: jq defaults for external data defense, registry needs file locking, hive wisdom trimmed before local context
Phase 4 (Autopilot) completed. Key patterns: separate run-state from colony-state, guard modulo ops against zero, gate counter increments by action type
Avoid repeating failure: Resilience issue found: Cross-repo lock isolation mismatch (high)
Learning captured: Hub-level shared files need hub-level locks — per-repo LOCK_DIR cannot protect cross-repo resources
Learning captured: Content sanitization pattern from pheromone-write is reusable for any user-supplied text input

Phase 1 (Hive Directory and Schema Foundation) completed. Key patterns: hub-level lock isolation for shared files, sanitization pattern reuse from pheromone-write, atomic_write for all hub-level JSON mutations
Learning captured: Orchestrator commands should delegate to sibling subcommands via bash $0 rather than duplicating logic
Learning captured: Text transformation placeholders should avoid angle brackets to prevent double-escaping
Phase 2 (Abstraction Layer) completed. Key patterns: orchestrator delegation via bash $0, path/version/branch stripping regex, pipeline error propagation
Learning captured: Domain filtering in colony-prime uses hive-read with domain_tags from registry
Learning captured: Chaos resilience strong: 5 scenarios tested, hive promotion handles empty instincts, special chars in paths, boundary confidence values correctly
Learning captured: Archaeology pre-build scans catch API contract mismatches (--instinct vs --text) that would silently fail at runtime
Learning captured: Dead code variables (declared but never used) should be caught by auditor and either removed or implemented
Phase 4 (Seal Promotion Hook) completed. Key patterns: archaeology pre-build scans catch API mismatches, dead code variables should be flagged by auditor
Avoid repeating failure: Resilience issue found: String-typed confidence causes silent hive-read failure (medium)
Learning captured: Confidence tier calculations should be inside atomic jq pipelines to preserve lock safety
Phase 5 (Multi-Repo Validation) completed. Key patterns: confidence tiers inside atomic jq pipelines, never-downgrade via max(), awk for float math
Learning captured: Documentation chaos testing catches gaps between documented behavior and actual code
Phase 6 (Documentation and Validation) completed. Key patterns: chaos testing docs against code catches accuracy gaps, 38/38 hive tests pass
Learning captured: Chaos resilience moderate: confidence cap correct, special chars safe, zero-instincts seal works. Medium: unbound var crash on missing flag values
Learning captured: Archaeologist pre-build scans catch latent bugs in recently-added code
Learning captured: When fixing one bug check surrounding lines for related bugs from the same commit
Phase 1 (Fix Wisdom Plumbing) completed. Key patterns: archaeologist pre-scan catches latent bugs, trap-based locking prevents deadlocks, when-when stutter fixed across 4 sites
Learning captured: Combining related subcommands into a single builder produces consistent APIs
Phase 2 (Add Midden Review) completed. Key patterns: single builder for related subcommands, trap-based locking, 6 tests
Learning captured: Extracting case blocks to functions requires changing positional parameter references
Phase 3 (Extract Monolith) completed. Key patterns: source-and-delegate with function wrappers, positional param audit needed after extraction
Learning captured: Watcher cross-checking catches stale number references in secondary doc locations
Phase 4 (Clean Up Documentation) completed. Key patterns: parallel doc builders for independent files, Watcher catches stale numbers in secondary locations
Phase 5 (Version Bump) completed. All 5 phases complete — v2.0.0 on hub
Learning captured: Process substitution avoids subshell variable loss in bash while loops
Learning captured: TDD with parallel background agents speeds up Phase 1 type work
[decision] Phase 1 complete — skills engine built with 10 functions, 24 tests. Advancing to Phase 2: Skill Content Authoring
Learning captured: Watcher independently catches state-path bugs that builders miss during refactoring
Learning captured: Separate infrastructure from state using distinct directory variables when moving scripts
Learning captured: Anchor .npmignore patterns with leading slash when directory name appears at multiple depths

Phase 1 (Move oracle infrastructure) completed. Key patterns: separate infrastructure from state with distinct dir variables, anchor .npmignore with leading slash, Watcher catches path bugs builders miss
Learning captured: SCRIPT_DIR references in test harness setup are easily missed when updating main path constants — grep separately
Phase 2 (Update oracle path references) completed. Key pattern: SCRIPT_DIR in test harness setups are separate from ORACLE_SH constants and easy to miss
Learning captured: Distribution pipeline has three independent exclusion layers — verify each separately
Phase 3 (Verify distribution pipeline) completed. All three exclusion layers verified independently
Learning captured: Position-aware path exclusion (first-segment-only) is needed when a directory name appears at multiple depths
Phase 4 (End-to-end validation) completed. All 4 phases done — oracle distribution fix verified end-to-end
Learning captured: Oracle research finding: What are the main components and their responsibilities in the Aether system? (85%)
Learning captured: Oracle research finding: What are the dependency relationships between components, and where are coupling risks? (82%)
Learning captured: Oracle research finding: Where are the risk areas — execution reliability gaps, silent failures, fake autonomy, hallucination vectors, and verification weaknesses? (82%)
Learning captured: Oracle research finding: How does the memory and context management system handle state persistence, session resume, context rot, and cross-colony knowledge sharing? (82%)

Learning captured: Oracle research finding: What would an expert change about this architecture to minimize user input while maximizing safety, verifiability, and autonomous execution capability? (80%)
Testing infrastructure present (69 test files) -- maintain TDD discipline
CI/CD pipeline active -- ensure changes pass all checks
Large codebase -- scope changes carefully, prefer incremental work
make sure the commit messages are useful and memorable not just numbers and digits
Testing infrastructure present (69 test files) — maintain TDD discipline
CI/CD pipeline active — ensure changes pass all checks
Large codebase — scope changes carefully, prefer incremental work
Visual consistency — use colony-visuals SKILL.md emoji reference map as canonical source
Inserted Phase 5: Add caste emojis to all ant spawn announcements and phase header displays across all commands — every spawn shows its caste emoji and phase headers include visual emoji markers

Learning captured: Inserted phase 5 (Stabilize caste emojis in spawn and phase displays): Add caste emojis to all ant spawn announcements and phase header displays across all commands — every spawn shows its caste emoji and phase headers include visual emoji markers
This is an AUDIT colony — verify, do not modify. Read-only investigation unless a bug is found.
aim to bring back the emojis and stuff that we had because things look dull now
Learning captured: Chaos resilience moderate: 5 scenarios tested on pheromone-expire date fix, 3 resilient, 2 findings (static fallback staleness, double date failure)
Minor note: pheromone-xml.sh may be missing from exchange folder — non-critical, only affects XML signal import/export
Learning captured: Test fixtures with hardcoded dates will break as calendar time advances — use dynamic date computation with cross-platform fallbacks instead
Phase 1 (Fix test 42) completed. Key pattern: hardcoded test dates break over time — use dynamic cross-platform date computation
Learning captured: Shell functions that set traps must compose with _aether_exit_cleanup to avoid orphaning file locks and temp files when the function exits abnormally
Learning captured: Shell functions that embed user-derived values in JSON output strings must use jq for safe construction to prevent JSON injection from special characters

Phase 2 (Verify today new test files and implementations) completed. Key patterns: shell trap composition must include _aether_exit_cleanup; JSON output construction must use jq for user-derived values
Learning captured: Weld-12 pipeline test: wisdom pipeline chain verification pattern
Learning captured: Continue-verify test observation

Learning captured: Playbook commands that chain generate-ant-name into spawn-log must parse the JSON result to extract the name — passing raw JSON corrupts spawn-tree.txt and breaks downstream parsers
Learning captured: Shell scripts building JSON output must escape all string fields including name and parent to prevent injection from special characters
Phase 3 (Audit QUEEN.md and wisdom pipeline integrity) completed. Key patterns: playbook commands must parse generate-ant-name JSON before passing to spawn-log; all JSON string fields need escaping
Learning captured: OpenCode entomb.md is missing several Claude-only features — functional parity is partial
Phase 4 (Verify colony lifecycle commands) completed. Key finding: entomb.md OpenCode version missing wisdom approval, eternal memory, expanded archival vs Claude version
Avoid repeating failure: Resilience issue found: JSON objects stored as ant names break spawn-tree JSON output (critical)
Avoid repeating failure: Resilience issue found: spawn-tree-load passes raw output without validation (high)
spawn-tree.sh: all string fields must be JSON-escaped before interpolation into JSON output
Never capture generate-ant-name output without piping through jq -r .result
Avoid repeating failure: Resilience issue found: Newline chars in queen-promote destroy QUEEN.md (critical)
Learning captured: Shell functions using sed c-command for line replacement must use head/tail instead to handle multi-line content safely on macOS BSD sed
Learning captured: Every atomic mv that overwrites a critical file must have a non-empty size guard to prevent data destruction from upstream pipeline failures
Phase 3 (Audit QUEEN.md and wisdom pipeline integrity) completed. Key patterns: sed c-command must use head/tail for newline safety; all atomic mv operations need empty-file guards

Learning captured: Lifecycle commands (init, seal, entomb) properly handle colony_version through template system — init uses colony-state.template.json, seal has 12 references, entomb reads and displays it
Learning captured: JSON injection in spawn.sh persists despite being identified as an instinct — pattern recurs because the fix was applied only to queen.sh, not spawn.sh
Phase 4 (Verify colony lifecycle commands) completed. Key patterns: lifecycle commands properly handle colony_version through template system, command parity maintained across Claude/OpenCode
Avoid repeating failure: Resilience issue found: All 9 spawn-tree tests time out due to O(n^2) parse_spawn_tree scaling (critical)
Avoid repeating failure: Resilience issue found: spawn-tree-active test passes for wrong reason — 5-pipe fixture format bypasses logic (high)

Inserted Phase 6: Fix all 3 spawn-tree blockers: (1) Fix O(n^2) parse_spawn_tree scaling that causes test timeouts, (2) JSON-escape ant names before interpolation into JSON output strings, (3) Add structural JSON validation in spawn-tree-load before passing through json_ok
Minimal changes only — smallest possible fix, no refactoring beyond what is needed. All currently-passing tests must continue to pass.

Learning captured: Inserted phase 6 (Stabilize spawn-tree parsing and JSON output): Fix all 3 spawn-tree blockers: (1) Fix O(n^2) parse_spawn_tree scaling that causes test timeouts, (2) JSON-escape ant names before interpolation into JSON output strings, (3) Add structural JSON validation in spawn-tree-load before passing through json_ok
Learning captured: Chaos resilience strong: 4/5 scenarios resilient for spawn-tree awk rewrite, 1 medium finding on newline edge case
Learning captured: Replacing bash while-read+sed loops with single-pass awk eliminates O(n^2) subprocess forking
Learning captured: awk NF==7 for spawn detection and $3 match for completion detection handles pipes-in-summary edge cases
Phase 6 (Stabilize spawn-tree parsing and JSON output) completed. Key patterns: single-pass awk eliminates O(n^2) subprocess forking, NF-based detection handles pipes-in-summary edge cases
Phase 7 (Audit summary and documentation) completed. Key pattern: audit report pass rates must be verified against live test output, not builder claims
Learning captured: Audit reports must use actual test output counts, not builder-claimed counts — always cross-verify bash test pass rates against live test-aether-utils.sh output
Testing infrastructure present (63 test files) -- maintain TDD discipline
Testing infrastructure present (64 test files) -- maintain TDD discipline
Testing infrastructure present (65 test files) -- maintain TDD discipline

Learning captured: Stage Audit Gate pattern: add pre-synthesis verification gate to build orchestrators ensures all stages complete before synthesis (evidence: all 6 success criteria passed, watcher quality 9/10)
Phase 1 (Enforce non-skippable playbook execution in build orchestrators) completed. Key patterns: pre-synthesis verification gates ensure all stages complete before synthesis
Phase 3 (Verify sync and lint clean) completed. Key patterns: dedicated lint verification phase as final gate confirms no sync drift across 45+45 commands and 24 agents
Site files built here must be transferred to the aetherantcolony.com repo at the end — build all 5 phases, transfer later
Phase 1 (GitHub Pages Site Foundation) completed. Key patterns: parallel wave pattern (create then modify), Auditor quality gate catches real issues (missing CSS, wrong year)
Phase 2 (Badge System Infrastructure) completed. Key pattern: .github/workflows/ is protected — Queen must create workflow files directly
Phase 3 (Spike Detection Pipeline) completed. All workflow files require Queen intervention for .github/workflows/ — established pattern
iterate until 100% certain we are only fixing something and not breaking anything
Phase 1 (Hazard Mapping and Baseline) completed. Key patterns: 4 failing tests prove DATA_DIR bug, contract assertion corrected, 51-site inventory classified (2 INTENTIONAL, 47 BUG)
Learning captured: Parallel swarm audits with 4 scouts are effective for commit review
Learning captured: Untracked test files can encode wrong assumptions about architecture
Phase 1 (Commit Audit) completed. Key patterns: parallel swarm audits effective for multi-area review; untracked files can encode wrong assumptions
Learning captured: Shell utility modules with pure calculation logic can be built independently by parallel builders
Learning captured: Builders that self-register dispatcher entries eliminate Wave 2 dependency
Phase 2 (Trust Scoring + Event Bus) completed. Parallel stateless module creation is effective; self-registration eliminates integration tasks
Learning captured: Additive modifications to existing files can safely parallel with new file creation
Phase 3 (Trust Storage + Graph) completed. Additive modifications safe for parallel builds
Learning captured: Curation modules with no shared state can be built in parallel then integrated by orchestrator
Phase 4 (Curation Ants) completed. 8 specialized ants with orchestrated execution order
Learning captured: Splitting related subcommands into separate files avoids parallel builder merge conflicts
Phase 5 (Consolidation Pipeline) completed. Lifecycle integration wired: phase-end at continue, full at seal
All 6 phases of Structural Learning Stack completed. Full pipeline operational.
[error-pattern] Category "resilience" recurring (3+ occurrences in midden)
[error-pattern] Category "quality" recurring (3+ occurrences in midden)
[error-pattern] Category "documentation" recurring (3+ occurrences in midden)
Avoid modifying files directly on the main branch during builds -- all changes go through PRs
Phase 2 (Trust Scoring Engine and Event Bus Foundation) completed. Pre-existing work verified: trust-scoring.sh (18 tests) and event-bus.sh (9 tests) both functional and registered in dispatcher.
[error-pattern] Category "documentation quality resilience security" recurring (6 6 12 3 occurrences)
Learning captured: Existing observations now recalculate trust_score on re-observation via trust-calculate with source_type/evidence_type/days_since (evidence: 34 tests pass, watcher quality 9/10)

Learning captured: Modifying existing code (learning.sh) and verifying new modules (graph.sh, instinct-store.sh) can safely run in parallel when modifications are additive (evidence: 3 parallel builders, 0 merge conflicts)
Learning captured: Standalone instinct storage with full schema is cleaner than embedding instincts in COLONY_STATE.json
Learning captured: jq-based graph layer provides adequate relationship traversal for instinct networks
Learning captured: Backward-compatible migration for existing observations lets new fields coexist with old data

Phase 3 (Trust-Scored Storage and Graph Layer) completed. Key patterns: standalone instinct storage with full schema is cleaner than embedding in colony state; backward-compatible migration with safe defaults works for adding fields to existing data
[error-pattern] Category "resilience" recurring (3+ occurrences in midden)
[error-pattern] Category "quality" recurring (3+ occurrences in midden)
[error-pattern] Category "documentation" recurring (3+ occurrences in midden)
Avoid modifying files directly on the main branch during builds -- all changes go through PRs
